create new article, with validation and success/errors context\n@app.route('/article/create', methods=['GET', 'POST'])\n@login_required\ndef create_article():\n    current_user = session['user']\n    errors = {}\n    success_flag = False\n    if request.method == 'POST':\n        form_data = request.form.to_dict()\n        # Additional backend validation for empty title\n        if not form_data.get('title') or not form_data.get('title').strip():\n            errors['title'] = 'Title cannot be empty.'\n        else:\n            success_flag, errors = article_service.create_article(current_user, form_data)\n        if success_flag:\n            flash('Article created successfully.', 'success')\n            return redirect(url_for('dashboard'))\n        else:\n            flash('Error creating article.', 'error')\n    return render_template('create_article.html',\n                           errors=errors,\n                           success_flag=success_flag)\n\n# Route: GET, POST /article/<article_id>/edit - edit existing article; passes article, errors, current_version\n@app.route('/article/<int:article_id>/edit', methods=['GET', 'POST'])\n@login_required\ndef edit_article(article_id):\n    current_user = session['user']\n    article, errors, current_version = article_service.get_article_and_version(article_id, current_user)\n    if article is None:\n        abort(404)\n    if request.method == 'POST':\n        form_data = request.form.to_dict()\n        success, form_errors = article_service.update_article(article_id, current_user, form_data)\n        # Replace errors dictionary to show form errors on frontend\n        if not errors:\n            errors = {}\n        errors.update(form_errors)\n        if success:\n            flash('Article updated successfully.', 'success')\n            return redirect(url_for('dashboard'))\n        else:\n            flash('Error updating article.', 'error')\n    return render_template('edit_article.html',\n                           article=article,\n                           errors=errors,\n                           current_version=current_version)\n\n# Route: GET /article/<article_id>/versions - fetch all versions for comparison/history display\n@app.route('/article/<int:article_id>/versions')\n@login_required\ndef article_version_history(article_id):\n    current_user = session['user']\n    versions = article_service.get_versions(article_id, current_user)\n    if versions is None:\n        abort(404)\n    comparison_data = article_service.get_comparison_data(article_id, current_user)\n    return render_template('article_versions.html',\n                           article_id=article_id,\n                           versions=versions,\n                           comparison_data=comparison_data)\n\n# Route: GET /articles/mine - user\u001as articles with optional status filter\n@app.route('/articles/mine')\n@login_required\ndef my_articles():\n    current_user = session['user']\n    filter_status = request.args.get('status')\n    articles = article_service.get_user_articles(current_user, filter_status)\n    filter_status_options = article_service.get_filter_status_options()\n    return render_template('my_articles.html',\n                           articles=articles,\n                           filter_status_options=filter_status_options)\n\n# Route: GET /articles/published - published articles listing with category and sort options\n@app.route('/articles/published')\ndef published_articles():\n    category = request.args.get('category')\n    sort = request.args.get('sort')\n    published_articles = article_service.list_published_articles(category, sort)\n    category_options = article_service.get_all_categories()\n    sort_options = article_service.get_sort_options()\n    return render_template('published_articles.html',\n                           published_articles=published_articles,\n                           category_options=category_options,\n                           sort_options=sort_options)\n\n# Route: GET /calendar - content calendar display with calendar data and view options\n@app.route('/calendar')\n@login_required\ndef content_calendar():\n    current_user = session['user']\n    calendar_data, view_options = article_service.get_content_calendar(current_user)\n    return render_template('content_calendar.html',\n                           calendar_data=calendar_data,\n                           view_options=view_options)\n\n# Route: GET /article/<article_id>/analytics - article performance analytics overview\n@app.route('/article/<int:article_id>/analytics')\n@login_required\ndef article_analytics(article_id):\n    current_user = session['user']\n    if not approval_service.can_view_article(current_user, article_id):\n        abort(403)\n    analytics_overview = analytics_service.get_overview(article_id)\n    total_views = analytics_service.get_total_views(article_id)\n    unique_visitors = analytics_service.get_unique_visitors(article_id)\n    return render_template('article_analytics.html',\n                           analytics_overview=analytics_overview,\n                           total_views=total_views,\n                           unique_visitors=unique_visitors)\n\n# Route: GET, POST /article/<article_id>/versions/<version_id>/comments - manage comments on article versions\n@app.route('/article/<int:article_id>/versions/<int:version_id>/comments', methods=['GET', 'POST'])\n@login_required\ndef comments_manage(article_id, version_id):\n    current_user = session['user']\n    if request.method == 'POST':\n        comment_text = request.form.get('comment')\n        if comment_text:\n            comment_service.add_comment(article_id, version_id, current_user, comment_text)\n            flash('Comment added.', 'success')\n        else:\n            flash('Comment text is required.', 'error')\n    comments = comment_service.get_comments(article_id, version_id)\n    return render_template('comments.html',\n                           article_id=article_id,\n                           version_id=version_id,\n                           comments=comments)\n\n# Route: POST /article/<article_id>/approve - handles the approval workflow for article versions\n@app.route('/article/<int:article_id>/approve', methods=['POST'])\n@login_required\ndef approve_article(article_id):\n    current_user = session['user']\n    version_id = request.form.get('version_id')\n    if not version_id:\n        abort(400)\n    success, message = approval_service.process_approval(article_id, int(version_id), current_user)\n    if success:\n        flash('Approval recorded.', 'success')\n    else:\n        flash(f'Approval failed: {message}', 'error')\n    return redirect(url_for('article_version_history', article_id=article_id))\n\n# Route: GET / - Redirects root to dashboard\n@app.route('/')\ndef root_redirect():\n    return redirect(url_for('dashboard'))\n\n# Route: GET, POST /login - user login management for session\n@app.route('/login', methods=['GET', 'POST'])\ndef login():\n    errors = {}\n    if request.method == 'POST':\n        username = request.form.get('username')\n        password = request.form.get('password')\n        if user_service.authenticate(username, password):\n            session['user'] = username\n            return redirect(url_for('dashboard'))\n        else:\n            errors['login'] = 'Invalid username or password'\n    return render_template('login.html', errors=errors)\n\n# Route: GET /logout - clears session and logs out user\n@app.route('/logout')\n@login_required\ndef logout():\n    session.clear()\n    return redirect(url_for('login'))\n\n# HTTP error handlers to render appropriate error templates\n@app.errorhandler(403)\ndef forbidden(e):\n    return render_template('403.html'), 403\n\n@app.errorhandler(404)\ndef not_found(e):\n    return render_template('404.html'), 404\n\n@app.errorhandler(500)\ndef internal_error(e):\n    return render_template('500.html'), 500\n\nif __name__ == '__main__':\n    app.run(debug=True)\n"})

from flask import Flask, request, render_template, redirect, url_for, session, abort, flash
from services.article_service import ArticleService
from services.user_service import UserService
from services.analytics_service import AnalyticsService
from services.approval_service import ApprovalService
from services.comment_service import CommentService

app = Flask(__name__)
app.secret_key = 'your_secret_key_here'

# Initialize service instances for separation of concerns and business logic
article_service = ArticleService()
user_service = UserService()
analytics_service = AnalyticsService()
approval_service = ApprovalService()
comment_service = CommentService()

# Decorator enforcing user session authentication
def login_required(func):
    from functools import wraps
    @wraps(func)
    def wrapper(*args, **kwargs):
        if 'user' not in session:
            return redirect(url_for('login'))
        return func(*args, **kwargs)
    return wrapper

# Route: GET /dashboard - shows user dashboard with quick stats and recent activity
@app.route('/dashboard')
@login_required
def dashboard():
    current_user = session['user']
    quick_stats = article_service.get_quick_stats(current_user)
    recent_activity = article_service.get_recent_activity(current_user)
    return render_template('dashboard.html',
                           username=current_user,
                           quick_stats=quick_stats,
                           recent_activity=recent_activity)

# Route: GET, POST /article/create - create new article, with validation and success/errors context
@app.route('/article/create', methods=['GET', 'POST'])
@login_required
def create_article():
    current_user = session['user']
    errors = {}
    success_flag = False
    if request.method == 'POST':
        form_data = request.form.to_dict()
        # Additional backend validation for empty title
        if not form_data.get('title') or not form_data.get('title').strip():
            errors['title'] = 'Title cannot be empty.'
        else:
            success_flag, errors = article_service.create_article(current_user, form_data)
        if success_flag:
            flash('Article created successfully.', 'success')
            return redirect(url_for('dashboard'))
        else:
            flash('Error creating article.', 'error')
    return render_template('create_article.html',
                           errors=errors,
                           success_flag=success_flag)

# Route: GET, POST /article/<article_id>/edit - edit existing article; passes article, errors, current_version
@app.route('/article/<int:article_id>/edit', methods=['GET', 'POST'])
@login_required
def edit_article(article_id):
    current_user = session['user']
    article, errors, current_version = article_service.get_article_and_version(article_id, current_user)
    if article is None:
        abort(404)
    if request.method == 'POST':
        form_data = request.form.to_dict()
        success, form_errors = article_service.update_article(article_id, current_user, form_data)
        # Replace errors dictionary to show form errors on frontend
        if not errors:
            errors = {}
        errors.update(form_errors)
        if success:
            flash('Article updated successfully.', 'success')
            return redirect(url_for('dashboard'))
        else:
            flash('Error updating article.', 'error')
    return render_template('edit_article.html',
                           article=article,
                           errors=errors,
                           current_version=current_version)

# Route: GET /article/<article_id>/versions - fetch all versions for comparison/history display
@app.route('/article/<int:article_id>/versions')
@login_required
def article_version_history(article_id):
    current_user = session['user']
    versions = article_service.get_versions(article_id, current_user)
    if versions is None:
        abort(404)
    comparison_data = article_service.get_comparison_data(article_id, current_user)
    return render_template('article_versions.html',
                           article_id=article_id,
                           versions=versions,
                           comparison_data=comparison_data)

# Route: GET /articles/mine - users articles with optional status filter
@app.route('/articles/mine')
@login_required
def my_articles():
    current_user = session['user']
    filter_status = request.args.get('status')
    articles = article_service.get_user_articles(current_user, filter_status)
    filter_status_options = article_service.get_filter_status_options()
    return render_template('my_articles.html',
                           articles=articles,
                           filter_status_options=filter_status_options)

# Route: GET /articles/published - published articles listing with category and sort options
@app.route('/articles/published')
def published_articles():
    category = request.args.get('category')
    sort = request.args.get('sort')
    published_articles = article_service.list_published_articles(category, sort)
    category_options = article_service.get_all_categories()
    sort_options = article_service.get_sort_options()
    return render_template('published_articles.html',
                           published_articles=published_articles,
                           category_options=category_options,
                           sort_options=sort_options)

# Route: GET /calendar - content calendar display with calendar data and view options
@app.route('/calendar')
@login_required
def content_calendar():
    current_user = session['user']
    calendar_data, view_options = article_service.get_content_calendar(current_user)
    return render_template('content_calendar.html',
                           calendar_data=calendar_data,
                           view_options=view_options)

# Route: GET /article/<article_id>/analytics - article performance analytics overview
@app.route('/article/<int:article_id>/analytics')
@login_required
def article_analytics(article_id):
    current_user = session['user']
    if not approval_service.can_view_article(current_user, article_id):
        abort(403)
    analytics_overview = analytics_service.get_overview(article_id)
    total_views = analytics_service.get_total_views(article_id)
    unique_visitors = analytics_service.get_unique_visitors(article_id)
    return render_template('article_analytics.html',
                           analytics_overview=analytics_overview,
                           total_views=total_views,
                           unique_visitors=unique_visitors)

# Route: GET, POST /article/<article_id>/versions/<version_id>/comments - manage comments on article versions
@app.route('/article/<int:article_id>/versions/<int:version_id>/comments', methods=['GET', 'POST'])
@login_required
def comments_manage(article_id, version_id):
    current_user = session['user']
    if request.method == 'POST':
        comment_text = request.form.get('comment')
        if comment_text:
            comment_service.add_comment(article_id, version_id, current_user, comment_text)
            flash('Comment added.', 'success')
        else:
            flash('Comment text is required.', 'error')
    comments = comment_service.get_comments(article_id, version_id)
    return render_template('comments.html',
                           article_id=article_id,
                           version_id=version_id,
                           comments=comments)

# Route: POST /article/<article_id>/approve - handles the approval workflow for article versions
@app.route('/article/<int:article_id>/approve', methods=['POST'])
@login_required
def approve_article(article_id):
    current_user = session['user']
    version_id = request.form.get('version_id')
    if not version_id:
        abort(400)
    success, message = approval_service.process_approval(article_id, int(version_id), current_user)
    if success:
        flash('Approval recorded.', 'success')
    else:
        flash(f'Approval failed: {message}', 'error')
    return redirect(url_for('article_version_history', article_id=article_id))

# Route: GET / - Redirects root to dashboard
@app.route('/')
def root_redirect():
    return redirect(url_for('dashboard'))

# Route: GET, POST /login - user login management for session
@app.route('/login', methods=['GET', 'POST'])
def login():
    errors = {}
    if request.method == 'POST':
        username = request.form.get('username')
        password = request.form.get('password')
        if user_service.authenticate(username, password):
            session['user'] = username
            return redirect(url_for('dashboard'))
        else:
            errors['login'] = 'Invalid username or password'
    return render_template('login.html', errors=errors)

# Route: GET /logout - clears session and logs out user
@app.route('/logout')
@login_required
def logout():
    session.clear()
    return redirect(url_for('login'))

# HTTP error handlers to render appropriate error templates
@app.errorhandler(403)
def forbidden(e):
    return render_template('403.html'), 403

@app.errorhandler(404)
def not_found(e):
    return render_template('404.html'), 404

@app.errorhandler(500)
def internal_error(e):
    return render_template('500.html'), 500

if __name__ == '__main__':
    app.run(debug=True)

<!DOCTYPE html>
<html lang="en">
<head>
    <meta charset="UTF-8" />
    <meta name="viewport" content="width=device-width, initial-scale=1" />
    <title>Create Article - ContentPublishingHub</title>

    <script>
        // Frontend validation to prevent empty title submission
        function validateForm(event) {
            var titleInput = document.getElementById('article-title');
            var errorSpan = document.getElementById('error-title');
            if (!titleInput.value.trim()) {
                event.preventDefault();
                if (!errorSpan) {
                    errorSpan = document.createElement('span');
                    errorSpan.id = 'error-title';
                    errorSpan.style.color = 'red';
                    titleInput.parentNode.insertBefore(errorSpan, titleInput.nextSibling);
                }
                errorSpan.textContent = 'Title cannot be empty.';
                titleInput.focus();
                return false;
            }
            // Clear error if title is valid
            if (errorSpan) {
                errorSpan.textContent = '';
            }
            return true;
        }

        window.addEventListener('DOMContentLoaded', function () {
            var form = document.getElementById('create-article-form');
            form.addEventListener('submit', validateForm);
        });
    </script>
</head>
<body>
    <div id="create-article-page">
        <h1>Create Article</h1>
        {% if success_flag %}
            <p id="success-message">Article created successfully!</p>
        {% endif %}

        <form id="create-article-form" method="post" action="{{ url_for('create_article') }}">
            <label for="article-title">Title:</label>
            <input type="text" id="article-title" name="title" value="{{ request.form.title if request.form.title else '' }}" required />
            {% if errors.title %}
                <span id="error-title" style="color:red">{{ errors.title }}</span>
            {% endif %}

            <label for="article-content">Content:</label>
            <textarea id="article-content" name="content" required>{{ request.form.content if request.form.content else '' }}</textarea>
            {% if errors.content %}
                <span id="error-content" style="color:red">{{ errors.content }}</span>
            {% endif %}

            <button type="submit" id="submit-article">Create Article</button>
        </form>

        <nav>
            <a href="{{ url_for('dashboard') }}" id="back-to-dashboard">Back to Dashboard</a>
        </nav>
    </div>
</body>
</html>

  [feedback_testing.txt] [NEED_MODIFY] Functional testing could not be fully validated due to environment constraints.  Defects found: - Defect 1: Create Article form allows submission with empty title, but backend validation is minimal.   [app.py] from flask import Flask, request, render_template, redirect, url_for, session, abort, flash from services.article_service import ArticleService from services.user_service import UserService from services.analytics_service import AnalyticsService from services.approval_service import ApprovalService from services.comment_service import CommentService  app = Flask(__name__) app.secret_key = 'your_secret_key_here'  # Initialize service instances for separation of concerns and business logic article_service = ArticleService() user_service = UserService() analytics_service = AnalyticsService() approval_service = ApprovalService() comment_service = CommentService()  # Decorator enforcing user session authentication def login_required(func):     from functools import wraps     @wraps(func)     def wrapper(*args, **kwargs):         if 'user' not in session:             return redirect(url_for('login'))         return func(*args, **kwargs)     return wrapper  # Route: GET /dashboard - shows user dashboard with quick stats and recent activity @app.route('/dashboard') @login_required def dashboard():     current_user = session['user']     quick_stats = article_service.get_quick_stats(current_user)     recent_activity = article_service.get_recent_activity(current_user)     return render_template('dashboard.html',                            username=current_user,                            quick_stats=quick_stats,                            recent_activity=recent_activity)  # Route: GET, POST /article/create - create new article, with validation and success/errors context @app.route('/article/create', methods=['GET', 'POST']) @login_required def create_article():     current_user = session['user']     errors = {}     success_flag = False     if request.method == 'POST':         form_data = request.form.to_dict()         # Additional backend validation for empty title         if not form_data.get('title') or not form_data.get('title').strip():             errors['title'] = 'Title cannot be empty.'         else:             success_flag, errors = article_service.create_article(current_user, form_data)         if success_flag:             flash('Article created successfully.', 'success')             return redirect(url_for('dashboard'))         else:             flash('Error creating article.', 'error')     return render_template('create_article.html',                            errors=errors,                            success_flag=success_flag)  # Route: GET, POST /article/<article_id>/edit - edit existing article; passes article, errors, current_version @app.route('/article/<int:article_id>/edit', methods=['GET', 'POST']) @login_required def edit_article(article_id):     current_user = session['user']     article, errors, current_version = article_service.get_article_and_version(article_id, current_user)     if article is None:         abort(404)     if request.method == 'POST':         form_data = request.form.to_dict()         success, form_errors = article_service.update_article(article_id, current_user, form_data)         # Replace errors dictionary to show form errors on frontend         if not errors:             errors = {}         errors.update(form_errors)         if success:             flash('Article updated successfully.', 'success')             return redirect(url_for('dashboard'))         else:             flash('Error updating article.', 'error')     return render_template('edit_article.html',                            article=article,                            errors=errors,                            current_version=current_version)  # Route: GET /article/<article_id>/versions - fetch all versions for comparison/history display @app.route('/article/<int:article_id>/versions') @login_required def article_version_history(article_id):     current_user = session['user']     versions = article_service.get_versions(article_id, current_user)     if versions is None:         abort(404)     comparison_data = article_service.get_comparison_data(article_id, current_user)     return render_template('article_versions.html',                            article_id=article_id,                            versions=versions,                            comparison_data=comparison_data)  # Route: GET /articles/mine - users articles with optional status filter @app.route('/articles/mine') @login_required def my_articles():     current_user = session['user']     filter_status = request.args.get('status')     articles = article_service.get_user_articles(current_user, filter_status)     filter_status_options = article_service.get_filter_status_options()     return render_template('my_articles.html',                            articles=articles,                            filter_status_options=filter_status_options)  # Route: GET /articles/published - published articles listing with category and sort options @app.route('/articles/published') def published_articles():     category = request.args.get('category')     sort = request.args.get('sort')     published_articles = article_service.list_published_articles(category, sort)     category_options = article_service.get_all_categories()     sort_options = article_service.get_sort_options()     return render_template('published_articles.html',                            published_articles=published_articles,                            category_options=category_options,                            sort_options=sort_options)  # Route: GET /calendar - content calendar display with calendar data and view options @app.route('/calendar') @login_required def content_calendar():     current_user = session['user']     calendar_data, view_options = article_service.get_content_calendar(current_user)     return render_template('content_calendar.html',                            calendar_data=calendar_data,                            view_options=view_options)  # Route: GET /article/<article_id>/analytics - article performance analytics overview @app.route('/article/<int:article_id>/analytics') @login_required def article_analytics(article_id):     current_user = session['user']     if not approval_service.can_view_article(current_user, article_id):         abort(403)     analytics_overview = analytics_service.get_overview(article_id)     total_views = analytics_service.get_total_views(article_id)     unique_visitors = analytics_service.get_unique_visitors(article_id)     return render_template('article_analytics.html',                            analytics_overview=analytics_overview,                            total_views=total_views,                            unique_visitors=unique_visitors)  # Route: GET, POST /article/<article_id>/versions/<version_id>/comments - manage comments on article versions @app.route('/article/<int:article_id>/versions/<int:version_id>/comments', methods=['GET', 'POST']) @login_required def comments_manage(article_id, version_id):     current_user = session['user']     if request.method == 'POST':         comment_text = request.form.get('comment')         if comment_text:             comment_service.add_comment(article_id, version_id, current_user, comment_text)             flash('Comment added.', 'success')         else:             flash('Comment text is required.', 'error')     comments = comment_service.get_comments(article_id, version_id)     return render_template('comments.html',                            article_id=article_id,                            version_id=version_id,                            comments=comments)  # Route: POST /article/<article_id>/approve - handles the approval workflow for article versions @app.route('/article/<int:article_id>/approve', methods=['POST']) @login_required def approve_article(article_id):     current_user = session['user']     version_id = request.form.get('version_id')     if not version_id:         abort(400)     success, message = approval_service.process_approval(article_id, int(version_id), current_user)     if success:         flash('Approval recorded.', 'success')     else:         flash(f'Approval failed: {message}', 'error')     return redirect(url_for('article_version_history', article_id=article_id))  # Route: GET / - INPUT: === feedback_testing.txt ===

[NEED_MODIFY] Functional testing could not be fully validated due to environment constraints.

Defects found:
- Defect 1: Create Article form allows submission with empty title, but backend validation is minimal.
  Specification: Frontend should prevent submission with empty title to reduce server load and enhance UX.
- Defect 2: Edit Article page does not display validation errors clearly for content field.
  Specification: Validation errors for content field should appear directly below the textarea in red color for better user feedback.
- Defect 3: Analytics page lists overview keys without labels, making some data unclear.
  Specification: Add descriptive labels beside values in analytics overview for clarity.

[NEED_MODIFY]

=== app.py ===
from flask import Flask, request, render_template, redirect, url_for, session, abort, flash
from services.article_service import ArticleService
from services.user_service import UserService
from services.analytics_service import AnalyticsService
from services.approval_service import ApprovalService
from services.comment_service import CommentService

app = Flask(__name__)
app.secret_key = 'your_secret_key_here'

# Initialize service instances for separation of concerns and business logic
article_service = ArticleService()
user_service = UserService()
analytics_service = AnalyticsService()
approval_service = ApprovalService()
comment_service = CommentService()

# Decorator enforcing user session authentication
def login_required(func):
    from functools import wraps
    @wraps(func)
    def wrapper(*args, **kwargs):
        if 'user' not in session:
            return redirect(url_for('login'))
        return func(*args, **kwargs)
    return wrapper

# Route: GET /dashboard - shows user dashboard with quick stats and recent activity
@app.route('/dashboard')
@login_required
def dashboard():
    current_user = session['user']
    quick_stats = article_service.get_quick_stats(current_user)
    recent_activity = article_service.get_recent_activity(current_user)
    return render_template('dashboard.html',
                           username=current_user,
                           quick_stats=quick_stats,
                           recent_activity=recent_activity)

# Route: GET, POST /article/create - create new article, with validation and success/errors context
@app.route('/article/create', methods=['GET', 'POST'])
@login_required
def create_article():
    current_user = session['user']
    errors = {}
    success_flag = False
    if request.method == 'POST':
        form_data = request.form.to_dict()
        # Additional backend validation for empty title
        if not form_data.get('title') or not form_data.get('title').strip():
            errors['title'] = 'Title cannot be empty.'
        else:
            success_flag, errors = article_service.create_article(current_user, form_data)
        if success_flag:
            flash('Article created successfully.', 'success')
            return redirect(url_for('dashboard'))
        else:
            flash('Error creating article.', 'error')
    return render_template('create_article.html',
                           errors=errors,
                           success_flag=success_flag)

# Route: GET, POST /article/<article_id>/edit - edit existing article; passes article, errors, current_version
@app.route('/article/<int:article_id>/edit', methods=['GET', 'POST'])
@login_required
def edit_article(article_id):
    current_user = session['user']
    article, errors, current_version = article_service.get_article_and_version(article_id, current_user)
    if article is None:
        abort(404)
    if request.method == 'POST':
        form_data = request.form.to_dict()
        success, form_errors = article_service.update_article(article_id, current_user, form_data)
        # Replace errors dictionary to show form errors on frontend
        if not errors:
            errors = {}
        errors.update(form_errors)
        if success:
            flash('Article updated successfully.', 'success')
            return redirect(url_for('dashboard'))
        else:
            flash('Error updating article.', 'error')
    return render_template('edit_article.html',
                           article=article,
                           errors=errors,
                           current_version=current_version)

# Route: GET /article/<article_id>/versions - fetch all versions for comparison/history display
@app.route('/article/<int:article_id>/versions')
@login_required
def article_version_history(article_id):
    current_user = session['user']
    versions = article_service.get_versions(article_id, current_user)
    if versions is None:
        abort(404)
    comparison_data = article_service.get_comparison_data(article_id, current_user)
    return render_template('article_versions.html',
                           article_id=article_id,
                           versions=versions,
                           comparison_data=comparison_data)

# Route: GET /articles/mine - users articles with optional status filter
@app.route('/articles/mine')
@login_required
def my_articles():
    current_user = session['user']
    filter_status = request.args.get('status')
    articles = article_service.get_user_articles(current_user, filter_status)
    filter_status_options = article_service.get_filter_status_options()
    return render_template('my_articles.html',
                           articles=articles,
                           filter_status_options=filter_status_options)

# Route: GET /articles/published - published articles listing with category and sort options
@app.route('/articles/published')
def published_articles():
    category = request.args.get('category')
    sort = request.args.get('sort')
    published_articles = article_service.list_published_articles(category, sort)
    category_options = article_service.get_all_categories()
    sort_options = article_service.get_sort_options()
    return render_template('published_articles.html',
                           published_articles=published_articles,
                           category_options=category_options,
                           sort_options=sort_options)

# Route: GET /calendar - content calendar display with calendar data and view options
@app.route('/calendar')
@login_required
def content_calendar():
    current_user = session['user']
    calendar_data, view_options = article_service.get_content_calendar(current_user)
    return render_template('content_calendar.html',
                           calendar_data=calendar_data,
                           view_options=view_options)

# Route: GET /article/<article_id>/analytics - article performance analytics overview
@app.route('/article/<int:article_id>/analytics')
@login_required
def article_analytics(article_id):
    current_user = session['user']
    if not approval_service.can_view_article(current_user, article_id):
        abort(403)
    analytics_overview = analytics_service.get_overview(article_id)
    total_views = analytics_service.get_total_views(article_id)
    unique_visitors = analytics_service.get_unique_visitors(article_id)
    return render_template('article_analytics.html',
                           analytics_overview=analytics_overview,
                           total_views=total_views,
                           unique_visitors=unique_visitors)

# Route: GET, POST /article/<article_id>/versions/<version_id>/comments - manage comments on article versions
@app.route('/article/<int:article_id>/versions/<int:version_id>/comments', methods=['GET', 'POST'])
@login_required
def comments_manage(article_id, version_id):
    current_user = session['user']
    if request.method == 'POST':
        comment_text = request.form.get('comment')
        if comment_text:
            comment_service.add_comment(article_id, version_id, current_user, comment_text)
            flash('Comment added.', 'success')
        else:
            flash('Comment text is required.', 'error')
    comments = comment_service.get_comments(article_id, version_id)
    return render_template('comments.html',
                           article_id=article_id,
                           version_id=version_id,
                           comments=comments)

# Route: POST /article/<article_id>/approve - handles the approval workflow for article versions
@app.route('/article/<int:article_id>/approve', methods=['POST'])
@login_required
def approve_article(article_id):
    current_user = session['user']
    version_id = request.form.get('version_id')
    if not version_id:
        abort(400)
    success, message = approval_service.process_approval(article_id, int(version_id), current_user)
    if success:
        flash('Approval recorded.', 'success')
    else:
        flash(f'Approval failed: {message}', 'error')
    return redirect(url_for('article_version_history', article_id=article_id))

# Route: GET / - Redirects root to dashboard
@app.route('/')
def root_redirect():
    return redirect(url_for('dashboard'))

# Route: GET, POST /login - user login management for session
@app.route('/login', methods=['GET', 'POST'])
def login():
    errors = {}
    if request.method == 'POST':
        username = request.form.get('username')
        password = request.form.get('password')
        if user_service.authenticate(username, password):
            session['user'] = username
            return redirect(url_for('dashboard'))
        else:
            errors['login'] = 'Invalid username or password'
    return render_template('login.html', errors=errors)

# Route: GET /logout - clears session and logs out user
@app.route('/logout')
@login_required
def logout():
    session.clear()
    return redirect(url_for('login'))

# HTTP error handlers to render appropriate error templates
@app.errorhandler(403)
def forbidden(e):
    return render_template('403.html'), 403

@app.errorhandler(404)
def not_found(e):
    return render_template('404.html'), 404

@app.errorhandler(500)
def internal_error(e):
    return render_template('500.html'), 500

if __name__ == '__main__':
    app.run(debug=True)

<body>
    <div id="content-calendar-page">

<!DOCTYPE html>
<html lang="en">
<head>
    <meta charset="UTF-8" />
    <meta name="viewport" content="width=device-width, initial-scale=1" />
    <title>Create Article - ContentPublishingHub</title>

    <script>
        // Frontend validation to prevent empty title submission
        function validateForm(event) {
            var titleInput = document.getElementById('article-title');
            var errorSpan = document.getElementById('error-title');
            if (!titleInput.value.trim()) {
                event.preventDefault();
                if (!errorSpan) {
                    errorSpan = document.createElement('span');
                    errorSpan.id = 'error-title';
                    errorSpan.style.color = 'red';
                    titleInput.parentNode.insertBefore(errorSpan, titleInput.nextSibling);
                }
                errorSpan.textContent = 'Title cannot be empty.';
                titleInput.focus();
                return false;
            }
            // Clear error if title is valid
            if (errorSpan) {
                errorSpan.textContent = '';
            }
            return true;
        }

        window.addEventListener('DOMContentLoaded', function () {
            var form = document.getElementById('create-article-form');
            form.addEventListener('submit', validateForm);
        });
    </script>
</head>
<body>
    <div id="create-article-page">
        <h1>Create Article</h1>
        {% if success_flag %}
            <p id="success-message">Article created successfully!</p>
        {% endif %}

        <form id="create-article-form" method="post" action="{{ url_for('create_article') }}">
            <label for="article-title">Title:</label>
            <input type="text" id="article-title" name="title" value="{{ request.form.title if request.form.title else '' }}" required />
            {% if errors.title %}
                <span id="error-title" style="color:red">{{ errors.title }}</span>
            {% endif %}

            <label for="article-content">Content:</label>
            <textarea id="article-content" name="content" required>{{ request.form.content if request.form.content else '' }}</textarea>
            {% if errors.content %}
                <span id="error-content" style="color:red">{{ errors.content }}</span>
            {% endif %}

            <button type="submit" id="submit-article">Create Article</button>
        </form>

        <nav>
            <a href="{{ url_for('dashboard') }}" id="back-to-dashboard">Back to Dashboard</a>
        </nav>
    </div>
</body>
</html>

INPUT: === app.py ===
from flask import Flask, request, render_template, redirect, url_for, session, abort, flash
from services.article_service import ArticleService
from services.user_service import UserService
from services.analytics_service import AnalyticsService
from services.approval_service import ApprovalService
from services.comment_service import CommentService

app = Flask(__name__)
app.secret_key = 'your_secret_key_here'

# Initialize service instances for separation of concerns and business logic
article_service = ArticleService()
user_service = UserService()
analytics_service = AnalyticsService()
approval_service = ApprovalService()
comment_service = CommentService()

# Decorator enforcing user session authentication
def login_required(func):
    from functools import wraps
    @wraps(func)
    def wrapper(*args, **kwargs):
        if 'user' not in session:
            return redirect(url_for('login'))
        return func(*args, **kwargs)
    return wrapper

# Route: GET /dashboard - shows user dashboard with quick stats and recent activity
@app.route('/dashboard')
@login_required
def dashboard():
    current_user = session['user']
    quick_stats = article_service.get_quick_stats(current_user)
    recent_activity = article_service.get_recent_activity(current_user)
    return render_template('dashboard.html',
                           username=current_user,
                           quick_stats=quick_stats,
                           recent_activity=recent_activity)

# Route: GET, POST /article/create - create new article, with validation and success/errors context
@app.route('/article/create', methods=['GET', 'POST'])
@login_required
def create_article():
    current_user = session['user']
    errors = {}
    success_flag = False
    if request.method == 'POST':
        form_data = request.form.to_dict()
        # Additional backend validation for empty title
        if not form_data.get('title') or not form_data.get('title').strip():
            errors['title'] = 'Title cannot be empty.'
        else:
            success_flag, errors = article_service.create_article(current_user, form_data)
        if success_flag:
            flash('Article created successfully.', 'success')
            return redirect(url_for('dashboard'))
        else:
            flash('Error creating article.', 'error')
    return render_template('create_article.html',
                           errors=errors,
                           success_flag=success_flag)

# Route: GET, POST /article/<article_id>/edit - edit existing article; passes article, errors, current_version
@app.route('/article/<int:article_id>/edit', methods=['GET', 'POST'])
@login_required
def edit_article(article_id):
    current_user = session['user']
    article, errors, current_version = article_service.get_article_and_version(article_id, current_user)
    if article is None:
        abort(404)
    if request.method == 'POST':
        form_data = request.form.to_dict()
        success, form_errors = article_service.update_article(article_id, current_user, form_data)
        # Replace errors dictionary to show form errors on frontend
        if not errors:
            errors = {}
        errors.update(form_errors)
        if success:
            flash('Article updated successfully.', 'success')
            return redirect(url_for('dashboard'))
        else:
            flash('Error updating article.', 'error')
    return render_template('edit_article.html',
                           article=article,
                           errors=errors,
                           current_version=current_version)

# Route: GET /article/<article_id>/versions - fetch all versions for comparison/history display
@app.route('/article/<int:article_id>/versions')
@login_required
def article_version_history(article_id):
    current_user = session['user']
    versions = article_service.get_versions(article_id, current_user)
    if versions is None:
        abort(404)
    comparison_data = article_service.get_comparison_data(article_id, current_user)
    return render_template('article_versions.html',
                           article_id=article_id,
                           versions=versions,
                           comparison_data=comparison_data)

# Route: GET /articles/mine - user\u001as articles with optional status filter
@app.route('/articles/mine')
@login_required
def my_articles():
    current_user = session['user']
    filter_status = request.args.get('status')
    articles = article_service.get_user_articles(current_user, filter_status)
    filter_status_options = article_service.get_filter_status_options()
    return render_template('my_articles.html',
                           articles=articles,
                           filter_status_options=filter_status_options)

# Route: GET /articles/published - published articles listing with category and sort options
@app.route('/articles/published')
def published_articles():
    category = request.args.get('category')
    sort = request.args.get('sort')
    published_articles = article_service.list_published_articles(category, sort)
    category_options = article_service.get_all_categories()
    sort_options = article_service.get_sort_options()
    return render_template('published_articles.html',
                           published_articles=published_articles,
                           category_options=category_options,
                           sort_options=sort_options)

# Route: GET /calendar - content calendar display with calendar data and view options
@app.route('/calendar')
@login_required
def content_calendar():
    current_user = session['user']
    calendar_data, view_options = article_service.get_content_calendar(current_user)
    return render_template('content_calendar.html',
                           calendar_data=calendar_data,
                           view_options=view_options)

# Route: GET /article/<article_id>/analytics - article performance analytics overview
@app.route('/article/<int:article_id>/analytics')
@login_required
def article_analytics(article_id):
    current_user = session['user']
    if not approval_service.can_view_article(current_user, article_id):
        abort(403)
    analytics_overview = analytics_service.get_overview(article_id)
    total_views = analytics_service.get_total_views(article_id)
    unique_visitors = analytics_service.get_unique_visitors(article_id)
    return render_template('article_analytics.html',
                           analytics_overview=analytics_overview,
                           total_views=total_views,
                           unique_visitors=unique_visitors)

# Route: GET, POST /article/<article_id>/versions/<version_id>/comments - manage comments on article versions
@app.route('/article/<int:article_id>/versions/<int:version_id>/comments', methods=['GET', 'POST'])
@login_required
def comments_manage(article_id, version_id):
    current_user = session['user']
    if request.method == 'POST':
        comment_text = request.form.get('comment')
        if comment_text:
            comment_service.add_comment(article_id, version_id, current_user, comment_text)
            flash('Comment added.', 'success')
        else:
            flash('Comment text is required.', 'error')
    comments = comment_service.get_comments(article_id, version_id)
    return render_template('comments.html',
                           article_id=article_id,
                           version_id=version_id,
                           comments=comments)

# Route: POST /article/<article_id>/approve - handles the approval workflow for article versions
@app.route('/article/<int:article_id>/approve', methods=['POST'])
@login_required
def approve_article(article_id):
    current_user = session['user']
    version_id = request.form.get('version_id')
    if not version_id:
        abort(400)
    success, message = approval_service.process_approval(article_id, int(version_id), current_user)
    if success:
        flash('Approval recorded.', 'success')
    else:
        flash(f'Approval failed: {message}', 'error')
    return redirect(url_for('article_version_history', article_id=article_id))

# Route: GET / - Redirects root to dashboard
@app.route('/')
def root_redirect():
    return redirect(url_for('dashboard'))

# Route: GET, POST /login - user login management for session
@app.route('/login', methods=['GET', 'POST'])
def login():
    errors = {}
    if request.method == 'POST':
        username = request.form.get('username')
        password = request.form.get('password')
        if user_service.authenticate(username, password):
            session['user'] = username
            return redirect(url_for('dashboard'))
        else:
            errors['login'] = 'Invalid username or password'
    return render_template('login.html', errors=errors)

# Route: GET /logout - clears session and logs out user
@app.route('/logout')
@login_required
def logout():
    session.clear()
    return redirect(url_for('login'))

# HTTP error handlers to render appropriate error templates
@app.errorhandler(403)
def forbidden(e):
    return render_template('403.html'), 403

@app.errorhandler(404)
def not_found(e):
    return render_template('404.html'), 404

@app.errorhandler(500)
def internal_error(e):
    return render_template('500.html'), 500

if __name__ == '__main__':
    app.run(debug=True)

<!DOCTYPE html>
<html lang="en">
<head>
    <meta charset="UTF-8" />
    <meta name="viewport" content="width=device-width, initial-scale=1" />
    <title>Create Article - ContentPublishingHub</title>

    <script>
        // Frontend validation to prevent empty title submission
        function validateForm(event) {
            var titleInput = document.getElementById('article-title');
            var errorSpan = document.getElementById('error-title');
            if (!titleInput.value.trim()) {
                event.preventDefault();
                if (!errorSpan) {
                    errorSpan = document.createElement('span');
                    errorSpan.id = 'error-title';
                    errorSpan.style.color = 'red';
                    titleInput.parentNode.insertBefore(errorSpan, titleInput.nextSibling);
                }
                errorSpan.textContent = 'Title cannot be empty.';
                titleInput.focus();
                return false;
            }
            // Clear error if title is valid
            if (errorSpan) {
                errorSpan.textContent = '';
            }
            return true;
        }

        window.addEventListener('DOMContentLoaded', function () {
            var form = document.getElementById('create-article-form');
            form.addEventListener('submit', validateForm);
        });
    </script>
</head>
<body>
    <div id="create-article-page">
        <h1>Create Article</h1>
        {% if success_flag %}
            <p id="success-message">Article created successfully!</p>
        {% endif %}

        <form id="create-article-form" method="post" action="{{ url_for('create_article') }}">
            <label for="article-title">Title:</label>
            <input type="text" id="article-title" name="title" value="{{ request.form.title if request.form.title else '' }}" required />
            {% if errors.title %}
                <span id="error-title" style="color:red">{{ errors.title }}</span>
            {% endif %}

            <label for="article-content">Content:</label>
            <textarea id="article-content" name="content" required>{{ request.form.content if request.form.content else '' }}</textarea>
            {% if errors.content %}
                <span id="error-content" style="color:red">{{ errors.content }}</span>
            {% endif %}

            <button type="submit" id="submit-article">Create Article</button>
        </form>

        <nav>
            <a href="{{ url_for('dashboard') }}" id="back-to-dashboard">Back to Dashboard</a>
        </nav>
    </div>
</body>
</html>

<!DOCTYPE html>
<html lang="en">

                        <a href="{{ url_for('edit_article', article_id=article_id) }}" id="edit-version-{{ version.version_id }}">Edit</a>
                        <a href="#" id="compare-version-{{ version.version_id }}">Compare</a>
                    </td>
                </tr>
                {% endfor %}
            </tbody>
        </table>

            <h2>Version Comparison</h2>

        </section>

  [app.py] from flask import Flask, request, render_template, redirect, url_for, session, abort, flash from services.article_service import ArticleService from services.user_service import UserService from services.analytics_service import AnalyticsService from services.approval_service import ApprovalService from services.comment_service import CommentService  app = Flask(__name__) app.secret_key = 'your_secret_key_here'  # Initialize service instances for separation of concerns and business logic article_service = ArticleService() user_service = UserService() analytics_service = AnalyticsService() approval_service = ApprovalService() comment_service = CommentService()  # Decorator enforcing user session authentication def login_required(func):     from functools import wraps     @wraps(func)     def wrapper(*args, **kwargs):         if 'user' not in session:             return redirect(url_for('login'))         return func(*args, **kwargs)     return wrapper  # Route: GET /dashboard - shows user dashboard with quick stats and recent activity @app.route('/dashboard') @login_required def dashboard():     current_user = session['user']     quick_stats = article_service.get_quick_stats(current_user)     recent_activity = article_service.get_recent_activity(current_user)     return render_template('dashboard.html',                            username=current_user,                            quick_stats=quick_stats,                            recent_activity=recent_activity)  # Route: GET, POST /article/create - create new article, with validation and success/errors context @app.route('/article/create', methods=['GET', 'POST']) @login_required def create_article():     current_user = session['user']     errors = {}     success_flag = False     if request.method == 'POST':         form_data = request.form.to_dict()         # Additional backend validation for empty title         if not form_data.get('title') or not form_data.get('title').strip():             errors['title'] = 'Title cannot be empty.'         else:             success_flag, errors = article_service.create_article(current_user, form_data)         if success_flag:             flash('Article created successfully.', 'success')             return redirect(url_for('dashboard'))         else:             flash('Error creating article.', 'error')     return render_template('create_article.html',                            errors=errors,                            success_flag=success_flag)  # Route: GET, POST /article/<article_id>/edit - edit existing article; passes article, errors, current_version @app.route('/article/<int:article_id>/edit', methods=['GET', 'POST']) @login_required def edit_article(article_id):     current_user = session['user']     article, errors, current_version = article_service.get_article_and_version(article_id, current_user)     if article is None:         abort(404)     if request.method == 'POST':         form_data = request.form.to_dict()         success, form_errors = article_service.update_article(article_id, current_user, form_data)         # Replace errors dictionary to show form errors on frontend         if not errors:             errors = {}         errors.update(form_errors)         if success:             flash('Article updated successfully.', 'success')             return redirect(url_for('dashboard'))         else:             flash('Error updating article.', 'error')     return render_template('edit_article.html',                            article=article,                            errors=errors,                            current_version=current_version)  # Route: GET /article/<article_id>/versions - fetch all versions for comparison/history display @app.route('/article/<int:article_id>/versions') @login_required def article_version_history(article_id):     current_user = session['user']     versions = article_service.get_versions(article_id, current_user)     if versions is None:         abort(404)     comparison_data = article_service.get_comparison_data(article_id, current_user)     return render_template('article_versions.html',                            article_id=article_id,                            versions=versions,                            comparison_data=comparison_data)  # Route: GET /articles/mine - user\u001as articles with optional status filter @app.route('/articles/mine') @login_required def my_articles():     current_user = session['user']     filter_status = request.args.get('status')     articles = article_service.get_user_articles(current_user, filter_status)     filter_status_options = article_service.get_filter_status_options()     return render_template('my_articles.html',                            articles=articles,                            filter_status_options=filter_status_options)  # Route: GET /articles/published - published articles listing with category and sort options @app.route('/articles/published') def published_articles():     category = request.args.get('category')     sort = request.args.get('sort')     published_articles = article_service.list_published_articles(category, sort)     category_options = article_service.get_all_categories()     sort_options = article_service.get_sort_options()     return render_template('published_articles.html',                            published_articles=published_articles,                            category_options=category_options,                            sort_options=sort_options)  # Route: GET /calendar - content calendar display with calendar data and view options @app.route('/calendar') @login_required def content_calendar():     current_user = session['user']     calendar_data, view_options = article_service.get_content_calendar(current_user)     return render_template('content_calendar.html',                            calendar_data=calendar_data,                            view_options=view_options)  # Route: GET /article/<article_id>/analytics - article performance analytics overview @app.route('/article/<int:article_id>/analytics') @login_required def article_analytics(article_id):     current_user = session['user']     if not approval_service.can_view_article(current_user, article_id):         abort(403)     analytics_overview = analytics_service.get_overview(article_id)     total_views = analytics_service.get_total_views(article_id)     unique_visitors = analytics_service.get_unique_visitors(article_id)     return render_template('article_analytics.html',                            analytics_overview=analytics_overview,                            total_views=total_views,                            unique_visitors=unique_visitors)  # Route: GET, POST /article/<article_id>/versions/<version_id>/comments - manage comments on article versions @app.route('/article/<int:article_id>/versions/<int:version_id>/comments', methods=['GET', 'POST']) @login_required def comments_manage(article_id, version_id):     current_user = session['user']     if request.method == 'POST':         comment_text = request.form.get('comment')         if comment_text:             comment_service.add_comment(article_id, version_id, current_user, comment_text)             flash('Comment added.', 'success')         else:             flash('Comment text is required.', 'error')     comments = comment_service.get_comments(article_id, version_id)     return render_template('comments.html',                            article_id=article_id,                            version_id=version_id,                            comments=comments)  # Route: POST /article/<article_id>/approve - handles the approval workflow for article versions @app.route('/article/<int:article_id>/approve', methods=['POST']) @login_required def approve_article(article_id):     current_user = session['user']     version_id = request.form.get('version_id')     if not version_id:         abort(400)     success, message = approval_service.process_approval(article_id, int(version_id), current_user)     if success:         flash('Approval recorded.', 'success')     else:         flash(f'Approval failed: {message}', 'error')     return redirect(url_for('article_version_history', article_id=article_id))  # Route: GET / - Redirects root to dashboard @app.route('/') def root_redirect():     return redirect(url_for('dashboard'))  # Route: GET, POST /login - user login management for session @app.route('/login', methods=['GET', 'POST']) def login():     errors = {}     if request.method == 'POST':         username = request.form.get('username')         password = request.form.get('password')         if user_service.authenticate(username, password):             session['user'] = username             return redirect(url_for('dashboard'))         else:             errors['login'] = 'Invalid username or password'     return render_template('login.html', errors=errors)  # Route: GET /logout - clears session and logs out user @app.route('/logout') @login_required def logout():     session.clear()     return redirect(url_for('login'))  # HTTP error handlers to render appropriate error templates @app.errorhandler(403) def forbidden(e):     return render_template('403.html'), 403  @app.errorhandler(404) def not_found(e):     return render_template('404.html'), 404  @app.errorhandler(500) def internal_error(e):     return render_template('500.html'), 500  if __name__ == '__main__':     app.run(debug=True)

INPUT: === feedback_testing.txt ===

[NEED_MODIFY] Functional testing could not be fully validated due to environment constraints.

Defects found:
- Defect 1: Create Article form allows submission with empty title, but backend validation is minimal.
  Specification: Frontend should prevent submission with empty title to reduce server load and enhance UX.
- Defect 2: Edit Article page does not display validation errors clearly for content field.
  Specification: Validation errors for content field should appear directly below the textarea in red color for better user feedback.
- Defect 3: Analytics page lists overview keys without labels, making some data unclear.
  Specification: Add descriptive labels beside values in analytics overview for clarity.

[NEED_MODIFY]

=== app.py ===
from flask import Flask, request, render_template, redirect, url_for, session, abort, flash
from services.article_service import ArticleService
from services.user_service import UserService
from services.analytics_service import AnalyticsService
from services.approval_service import ApprovalService
from services.comment_service import CommentService

app = Flask(__name__)
app.secret_key = 'your_secret_key_here'

# Initialize service instances for separation of concerns and business logic
article_service = ArticleService()
user_service = UserService()
analytics_service = AnalyticsService()
approval_service = ApprovalService()
comment_service = CommentService()

# Decorator enforcing user session authentication
def login_required(func):
    from functools import wraps
    @wraps(func)
    def wrapper(*args, **kwargs):
        if 'user' not in session:
            return redirect(url_for('login'))
        return func(*args, **kwargs)
    return wrapper

# Route: GET /dashboard - shows user dashboard with quick stats and recent activity
@app.route('/dashboard')
@login_required
def dashboard():
    current_user = session['user']
    quick_stats = article_service.get_quick_stats(current_user)
    recent_activity = article_service.get_recent_activity(current_user)
    return render_template('dashboard.html',
                           username=current_user,
                           quick_stats=quick_stats,
                           recent_activity=recent_activity)

# Route: GET, POST /article/create - create new article, with validation and success/errors context
@app.route('/article/create', methods=['GET', 'POST'])
@login_required
def create_article():
    current_user = session['user']
    errors = {}
    success_flag = False
    if request.method == 'POST':
        form_data = request.form.to_dict()
        # Additional backend validation for empty title
        if not form_data.get('title') or not form_data.get('title').strip():
            errors['title'] = 'Title cannot be empty.'
        else:
            success_flag, errors = article_service.create_article(current_user, form_data)
        if success_flag:
            flash('Article created successfully.', 'success')
            return redirect(url_for('dashboard'))
        else:
            flash('Error creating article.', 'error')
    return render_template('create_article.html',
                           errors=errors,
                           success_flag=success_flag)

# Route: GET, POST /article/<article_id>/edit - edit existing article; passes article, errors, current_version
@app.route('/article/<int:article_id>/edit', methods=['GET', 'POST'])
@login_required
def edit_article(article_id):
    current_user = session['user']
    article, errors, current_version = article_service.get_article_and_version(article_id, current_user)
    if article is None:
        abort(404)
    if request.method == 'POST':
        form_data = request.form.to_dict()
        success, form_errors = article_service.update_article(article_id, current_user, form_data)
        # Replace errors dictionary to show form errors on frontend
        if not errors:
            errors = {}
        errors.update(form_errors)
        if success:
            flash('Article updated successfully.', 'success')
            return redirect(url_for('dashboard'))
        else:
            flash('Error updating article.', 'error')
    return render_template('edit_article.html',
                           article=article,
                           errors=errors,
                           current_version=current_version)

# Route: GET /article/<article_id>/versions - fetch all versions for comparison/history display
@app.route('/article/<int:article_id>/versions')
@login_required
def article_version_history(article_id):
    current_user = session['user']
    versions = article_service.get_versions(article_id, current_user)
    if versions is None:
        abort(404)
    comparison_data = article_service.get_comparison_data(article_id, current_user)
    return render_template('article_versions.html',
                           article_id=article_id,
                           versions=versions,
                           comparison_data=comparison_data)

# Route: GET /articles/mine - user\u001as articles with optional status filter
@app.route('/articles/mine')
@login_required
def my_articles():
    current_user = session['user']
    filter_status = request.args.get('status')
    articles = article_service.get_user_articles(current_user, filter_status)
    filter_status_options = article_service.get_filter_status_options()
    return render_template('my_articles.html',
                           articles=articles,
                           filter_status_options=filter_status_options)

# Route: GET /articles/published - published articles listing with category and sort options
@app.route('/articles/published')
def published_articles():
    category = request.args.get('category')
    sort = request.args.get('sort')
    published_articles = article_service.list_published_articles(category, sort)
    category_options = article_service.get_all_categories()
    sort_options = article_service.get_sort_options()
    return render_template('published_articles.html',
                           published_articles=published_articles,
                           category_options=category_options,
                           sort_options=sort_options)

# Route: GET /calendar - content calendar display with calendar data and view options
@app.route('/calendar')
@login_required
def content_calendar():
    current_user = session['user']
    calendar_data, view_options = article_service.get_content_calendar(current_user)
    return render_template('content_calendar.html',
                           calendar_data=calendar_data,
                           view_options=view_options)

# Route: GET /article/<article_id>/analytics - article performance analytics overview
@app.route('/article/<int:article_id>/analytics')
@login_required
def article_analytics(article_id):
    current_user = session['user']
    if not approval_service.can_view_article(current_user, article_id):
        abort(403)
    analytics_overview = analytics_service.get_overview(article_id)
    total_views = analytics_service.get_total_views(article_id)
    unique_visitors = analytics_service.get_unique_visitors(article_id)
    return render_template('article_analytics.html',
                           analytics_overview=analytics_overview,
                           total_views=total_views,
                           unique_visitors=unique_visitors)

# Route: GET, POST /article/<article_id>/versions/<version_id>/comments - manage comments on article versions
@app.route('/article/<int:article_id>/versions/<int:version_id>/comments', methods=['GET', 'POST'])
@login_required
def comments_manage(article_id, version_id):
    current_user = session['user']
    if request.method == 'POST':
        comment_text = request.form.get('comment')
        if comment_text:
            comment_service.add_comment(article_id, version_id, current_user, comment_text)
            flash('Comment added.', 'success')
        else:
            flash('Comment text is required.', 'error')
    comments = comment_service.get_comments(article_id, version_id)
    return render_template('comments.html',
                           article_id=article_id,
                           version_id=version_id,
                           comments=comments)

# Route: POST /article/<article_id>/approve - handles the approval workflow for article versions
@app.route('/article/<int:article_id>/approve', methods=['POST'])
@login_required
def approve_article(article_id):
    current_user = session['user']
    version_id = request.form.get('version_id')
    if not version_id:
        abort(400)
    success, message = approval_service.process_approval(article_id, int(version_id), current_user)
    if success:
        flash('Approval recorded.', 'success')
    else:
        flash(f'Approval failed: {message}', 'error')
    return redirect(url_for('article_version_history', article_id=article_id))

# Route: GET / - Redirects root to dashboard
@app.route('/')
def root_redirect():
    return redirect(url_for('dashboard'))

# Route: GET, POST /login - user login management for session
@app.route('/login', methods=['GET', 'POST'])
def login():
    errors = {}
    if request.method == 'POST':
        username = request.form.get('username')
        password = request.form.get('password')
        if user_service.authenticate(username, password):
            session['user'] = username
            return redirect(url_for('dashboard'))
        else:
            errors['login'] = 'Invalid username or password'
    return render_template('login.html', errors=errors)

# Route: GET /logout - clears session and logs out user
@app.route('/logout')
@login_required
def logout():
    session.clear()
    return redirect(url_for('login'))

# HTTP error handlers to render appropriate error templates
@app.errorhandler(403)
def forbidden(e):
    return render_template('403.html'), 403

@app.errorhandler(404)
def not_found(e):
    return render_template('404.html'), 404

@app.errorhandler(500)
def internal_error(e):
    return render_template('500.html'), 500

if __name__ == '__main__':
    app.run(debug=True)

<!DOCTYPE html>
<html lang="en">
<head>
    <meta charset="UTF-8" />
    <meta name="viewport" content="width=device-width, initial-scale=1" />
    <title>Create Article - ContentPublishingHub</title>

    <script>
        // Frontend validation to prevent empty title submission
        function validateForm(event) {
            var titleInput = document.getElementById('article-title');
            var errorSpan = document.getElementById('error-title');
            if (!titleInput.value.trim()) {
                event.preventDefault();
                if (!errorSpan) {
                    errorSpan = document.createElement('span');
                    errorSpan.id = 'error-title';
                    errorSpan.style.color = 'red';
                    titleInput.parentNode.insertBefore(errorSpan, titleInput.nextSibling);
                }
                errorSpan.textContent = 'Title cannot be empty.';
                titleInput.focus();
                return false;
            }
            // Clear error if title is valid
            if (errorSpan) {
                errorSpan.textContent = '';
            }
            return true;
        }

        window.addEventListener('DOMContentLoaded', function () {
            var form = document.getElementById('create-article-form');
            form.addEventListener('submit', validateForm);
        });
    </script>
</head>
<body>
    <div id="create-article-page">
        <h1>Create Article</h1>
        {% if success_flag %}
            <p id="success-message">Article created successfully!</p>
        {% endif %}

        <form id="create-article-form" method="post" action="{{ url_for('create_article') }}">
            <label for="article-title">Title:</label>
            <input type="text" id="article-title" name="title" value="{{ request.form.title if request.form.title else '' }}" required />
            {% if errors.title %}
                <span id="error-title" style="color:red">{{ errors.title }}</span>
            {% endif %}

            <label for="article-content">Content:</label>
            <textarea id="article-content" name="content" required>{{ request.form.content if request.form.content else '' }}</textarea>
            {% if errors.content %}
                <span id="error-content" style="color:red">{{ errors.content }}</span>
            {% endif %}

            <button type="submit" id="submit-article">Create Article</button>
        </form>

        <nav>
            <a href="{{ url_for('dashboard') }}" id="back-to-dashboard">Back to Dashboard</a>
        </nav>
    </div>
</body>
</html>

<!DOCTYPE html>
<html lang="en">
<head>
    <meta charset="UTF-8" />
    <meta name="viewport" content="width=device-width, initial-scale=1" />
    <title>My Articles - ContentPublishingHub</title>
